evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` and `src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts` both compute date-based import-budget constants at module load (`RUNTIME_ERROR_ALIAS_IMPORT_BUDGET`, `LEGACY_SUPABASE_IMPORT_BUDGET`), creating time-dependent import behavior.

`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts` uses runtime reflection/probing of `supabaseClientModule` (`getSupabaseClient` vs `supabase`) and fallback behavior, which blurs migration boundaries.

`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` coordinates cross-feature behavior through global app events (`useAppEventListener('selectShotForAddition', ...)`) and large stateful callback surface, while related gallery item/action files carry parallel UI-state orchestration.

`src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx` (shared primitive) depends on app-theme variant maps (`theme/buttonVariants`), and `src/shared/components/MediaTypeFilter.tsx` embeds app-specific variant/color decisions (`retro`, `zinc`) inside shared layer components.

Travel-between-images canonical types still embed deprecated fields by extending `LegacyStructureVideoFields` in `StructureVideoConfig` and by exposing `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput` as modern+legacy union (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts`).
Join-clips compatibility surface still accepts and normalizes legacy path/video_edit fields as active input modes (`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts`).

Generation row typing is split between new domain contract (`src/domains/generation/types/generationViewRow.ts`) and legacy barrel usage with mixed-shape helpers (`src/shared/lib/mediaTypeHelpers.ts`).
Legacy alias governance is inconsistent: Supabase legacy facade has deadline warning logic, while deprecated runtime error alias exports in `handleError.ts` have no usage pressure mechanism.

Lightbox stack still relies on very wide state/prop surfaces: `MediaLightboxProps` exposes ~55 fields and then re-bundles them (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/MediaLightbox.tsx:11-77`, `:79-147`), while downstream hooks/components keep large composite contracts (`useSharedLightboxState` return sections in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts:144-253`, `EditModePanelProps` in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/EditModePanel.tsx:27-98`).

Travel-between-images editor uses pass-through mega interfaces rather than bounded feature seams: `ShotEditorLayoutProps` carries many unrelated concerns and forwards them to multiple sections (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/ShotEditorLayout.tsx:8-83`, `:154-235`), while `ShotSettingsContextValue` aggregates broad domain state and handlers in one context (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/ShotSettingsContext.tsx:194-248`).

Supabase edge functions show scaffold drift: some handlers use shared runtime/body helpers (`complete-auto-topup-setup` via `initEdgeRuntime` and `parseJsonBodyStrictResponse`), while others manually re-implement env/client/body/auth/response plumbing (`claim-next-task`, `update-shot-pair-prompts`, `process-auto-topup`), increasing structural inconsistency across sibling endpoints.

findings.summary: Core compatibility modules execute time-based policy at import time, introducing non-deterministic module behavior.
evidence: `handleError.ts` exports `RUNTIME_ERROR_ALIAS_IMPORT_BUDGET = getRuntimeErrorAliasImportBudget()` where the default path calls `new Date()`.
`legacySupabaseFacade.ts` exports `LEGACY_SUPABASE_IMPORT_BUDGET = getLegacySupabaseImportBudget()` with the same date-at-import pattern.
findings.suggestion: Move budget evaluation behind explicit runtime functions or diagnostics-only entrypoints; export static policy tables and let callers pass `now` when needed. Avoid computed time-dependent constants during module initialization.

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase facade dynamically probes multiple runtime APIs, masking boundary drift and prolonging compatibility coupling.
evidence: `getLegacySupabaseClient()` checks for `getSupabaseClient`, then falls back to `supabase` via `Record<string, unknown>` casts and try/catch probing.
The file is explicitly marked deprecated with removal target, but still exposes a broad transitional behavior surface rather than a narrow, enforceable adapter.
`handleError.ts` also retains deprecated alias exports (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`), indicating a repeated pattern of long-lived compatibility surfaces in foundational modules.
findings.suggestion: Replace runtime probing with one explicit adapter contract (single accessor), gate by build-time/type-time API, and shift deprecation telemetry into a dedicated migration monitor module. Enforce sunset by failing CI on remaining legacy imports.

findings.summary: Gallery interaction flow is coordinated through global events plus distributed UI-state contracts, increasing cross-module coupling.
evidence: `useMediaGalleryHandlers` consumes many cross-cutting callbacks/state setters and subscribes to `useAppEventListener('selectShotForAddition', ...)`.
`ShotActions` defines multiple nested state/callback interfaces and coordinates navigation/add/mode behavior, indicating orchestration split across modules rather than a single bounded controller.
`useMediaGalleryItemState` carries additional local modal/tooltip/shot state and error routing, further distributing interaction policy.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a dedicated gallery interaction controller (context/store or reducer) with typed commands/events scoped to gallery domain; migrate components/hooks to consume this controller rather than global app events and wide callback props.

findings.summary: Shared UI primitives are coupled to app-theme decisions, weakening layer boundaries and reuse contracts.
evidence: `button.tsx` imports `APP_BUTTON_SIZES` and `APP_BUTTON_VARIANTS` from `shared/components/theme/buttonVariants` and merges them into primitive variants.
`MediaTypeFilter.tsx` hardcodes app-specific variant/colorScheme choices (`retro`, `retro-dark`, `zinc`) inside shared component layer.
Shared primitives (`label.tsx`/`button.tsx`) sit in the same layer as themed behavior, making dependency direction unclear between foundation UI and app branding.
findings.suggestion: Keep `shared/components/ui/*` as theme-agnostic primitives and move brand/theme composition into wrapper components (e.g., `ThemedButton`, `ThemedMediaTypeFilter`) in a higher layer.

findings.summary: Canonical travel-between-images types still allow deprecated structure-video fields, so migration boundary is not actually enforced.
evidence: `StructureVideoConfig` extends `LegacyStructureVideoFields`, which reintroduces deprecated keys like `motion_strength`, `structure_type`, and `uni3c_*` into the modern structure-video shape.

`TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput` is defined as `TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams & LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams`, while comments claim write path should emit only `structure_guidance` + `structure_videos`.
findings.suggestion: Split read/write contracts explicitly: keep a legacy read adapter type, but make canonical write interfaces exclude deprecated fields (`never`/omitted keys), then centralize normalization in one adapter module before task submission.

findings.summary: Join-clips compatibility adapter still treats legacy inputs as normal operational modes, extending migration life instead of constraining it.
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` still exposes extensive legacy fields (`starting_video_path`, `ending_video_path`, `video_edit_mode`, `source_*`, `portions_to_regenerate`) and supports `mode: 'legacy'`.
Adapter inference explicitly resolves `legacy_paths` and maps mode to `'legacy'`, then forwards canonical creation through `createCanonicalJoinClipsTask`.
`taskConfig` still carries hidden legacy processing types (e.g., `join_clips_segment`) as hardcoded fallback inventory, reinforcing old/new coexistence.
findings.suggestion: Gate legacy mode behind a feature flag or strict telemetry threshold, remove `'legacy'` from public compat params first, and force conversion at boundary utilities with explicit deprecation failure after the removal date.

findings.summary: Generation-row typing migration is incomplete, with new domain types and legacy barrel types both active, preserving mixed-shape handling.
evidence: `image-utils.ts` imports `GenerationRow` from new domain path `@/domains/generation/types/generationViewRow`.
`mediaTypeHelpers.ts` imports `GenerationRow` from legacy barrel `@/types/generationAndShots` and still includes compatibility logic prioritizing `generation_id > metadata.generation_id > id`.
`variantToGenerationRow` fabricates a row with both `id` and `generation_id` set from fallback logic, indicating unresolved schema/source-of-truth migration.
findings.suggestion: Converge all generation-row consumers on one canonical type module, then isolate legacy shape parsing to a single ingress adapter returning strict domain rows.

findings.summary: Deprecated alias surfaces use inconsistent enforcement, so some migrations get pressure while others can stall indefinitely.
evidence: Supabase facade includes runtime removal-target checks (`warnIfPastRemovalTarget`) and explicit migration messaging.
`handleError.ts` exports deprecated aliases (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) with removal metadata but no warning, usage logging, or gate when imported/called.
Both modules define import budget constants, but only one has runtime behavior to enforce migration pressure.
findings.suggestion: Standardize deprecation policy for alias facades: shared helper for deadline checks, warning/telemetry hooks, and optional hard-fail mode after removal target.

findings.summary: Task-type migration is incomplete because canonical DB-driven config still depends on legacy hardcoded fallback tables and heuristic matching.
evidence: `taskConfig.ts` describes DB as source of truth but keeps broad `HARDCODED_TASK_TYPE_CONFIG` fallback with old and hidden types.
`taskTypeUtils.ts` combines explicit sets with heuristic `taskType.includes('travel')`, which can mask unresolved migration naming and classification debt.
`taskIdHelpers.ts` still resolves multiple legacy task-id fields (`source_task_id`, `orchestrator_task_id`, `task_id`), indicating historic schema variants remain active in the core path.
findings.suggestion: Move to a strict canonical task-type registry contract: limit hardcoded fallback to minimal bootstrap set, remove substring heuristics, and deprecate multi-key task-id extraction once canonical fields are guaranteed.

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase facade depends on internal module symbol probing, weakening the boundary contract.
evidence: README.md defines the runtime contract around `initializeSupabase()`/`getSupabaseClient()` and legacy-shim governance.
In `getLegacySupabaseClient()`, the facade accesses `supabaseClientModule` via `Record<string, unknown>` and conditionally calls `getSupabaseClient` or falls back to `supabase` property.
This means behavior depends on internal export names rather than one stable interface, so refactors in `client.ts` can silently break legacy callers.
findings.suggestion: Replace symbol probing with a single explicit adapter contract exported by the runtime module (for example `getRuntimeSupabaseClientForLegacy()`), and have the legacy facade call only that contract. Keep fallback behavior, if needed, inside the runtime module so dependency direction stays explicit.

findings.summary: Deprecation governance logic is duplicated across modules instead of centralized.
evidence: `handleError.ts` defines `*_OWNER`, `*_REMOVE_BY`, phased import budgets, a date-based budget function, and alias exports.
`legacySupabaseFacade.ts` repeats the same governance structure with different constants and functions.
README.md separately encodes the same policy intent for both areas, creating three policy-definition locations (docs + 2 modules).
findings.suggestion: Create a shared governance contract module for deprecation policies (owner/removal target/phases/date evaluation), and have each alias module register metadata against that shared shape. Generate or reference README policy snippets from the same source to avoid docs/runtime drift.

findings.summary: Import-time budget constants in highly shared modules increase hidden policy coupling.
evidence: `RUNTIME_ERROR_ALIAS_IMPORT_BUDGET` and `LEGACY_SUPABASE_IMPORT_BUDGET` are computed at module import time from `new Date()`.
These files are high-import shared surfaces (not local leaf modules), so importing them also instantiates time-based policy state.
This couples unrelated consumers to governance timing concerns and makes policy semantics depend on module load timing.
findings.suggestion: Avoid exported import-time policy values in shared hubs; expose pure policy evaluators only (`get*ImportBudget(now)`), and have governance-check tooling call them directly with explicit dates. Keep runtime consumer modules free of date-derived governance state.

findings.summary: Lightbox split keeps a large shared contract, so decomposition reduced file size more than responsibility coupling.
evidence: `MediaLightboxProps` defines a very broad API (navigation, shot workflow, task details, trim, optimistic updates, segment-slot state) in one interface (`MediaLightbox.tsx:11-77`).
The `useBundledLightboxProps` hook repackages the same wide surface into grouped objects but does not reduce behavioral coupling (`MediaLightbox.tsx:79-147`).

`useSharedLightboxState` returns many nested capability groups in one hook result (`useSharedLightboxState.ts:144-253`), and `EditModePanel` still consumes a broad mixed prop contract (`EditModePanel.tsx:27-98`).
findings.suggestion: Introduce task-focused facades (`LightboxNavigationController`, `ShotWorkflowController`, `EditSessionController`) created near ownership boundaries, and pass those facades down instead of raw field bags. Keep each controller under a tight method/state budget and migrate components incrementally (MediaLightbox -> Image/VideoLightbox -> panel/layout children).

findings.summary: Shot editor composition relies on pass-through mega props plus mega context, blurring ownership boundaries.
evidence: `ShotEditorLayoutProps` includes large cross-cutting concerns (header behavior, timeline state, generation controls, modal state, refs) and forwards most of them into child sections (`ShotEditorLayout.tsx:8-83`, `:154-235`).

`ShotSettingsContextValue` centralizes many domains (shot data, structure video, audio, image handlers, generation mode, join state, dimensions, query client) into one context contract (`ShotSettingsContext.tsx:194-248`).

`useTimelineOrchestrator` exposes a very large return interface covering refs, dimensions, drag/drop, selection, pending state, endpoint drag, video-browser controls, and device logic (`useTimelineOrchestrator.ts:54-139`).
findings.suggestion: Split the editor into explicit bounded contexts (`timeline`, `generation`, `asset-management`, `modals`) and keep each section wired to only its own context slice. Replace the monolithic layout prop interface with section-specific view models built by a top-level coordinator.

findings.summary: Sibling edge functions use inconsistent runtime/request scaffolds, causing repeated orchestration boilerplate.
evidence: `claim-next-task` and `update-shot-pair-prompts` manually perform env checks, client creation, method gates, JSON parsing, auth, and response building (`claim-next-task/index.ts:41-87`, `update-shot-pair-prompts/index.ts:35-111`).
`process-auto-topup` defines local CORS/json helper and manual runtime bootstrapping (`process-auto-topup/index.ts:7-20`, `:61-84`).

`complete-auto-topup-setup` instead uses shared runtime/body abstractions (`initEdgeRuntime`, `parseJsonBodyStrictResponse`) (`complete-auto-topup-setup/index.ts:33-53`), showing partial adoption of a better pattern.
findings.suggestion: Standardize all edge handlers on a single `edgeRequest` pipeline (runtime init, method guard, auth policy, body parsing, response helpers, logger lifecycle). Migrate manual handlers to the shared wrapper and keep endpoint files focused on business logic only.

findings.summary: `complete_task/params.ts` mixes param-path migration logic with media utilities and payload mutation.
evidence: `params.ts` contains multiple unrelated responsibilities: path extraction fallback logic (`extractFromParams`), thumbnail-path mutation (`setThumbnailInParams`), MIME type detection (`getContentType`), and generation payload composition (`buildGenerationParams`) (`params.ts:33-260`).

The same module is consumed by both orchestration status flow and generation creation flow (`orchestrator.ts` imports extraction helpers at `:7`; `generation-child.ts` imports extraction and payload builders at `:17`), increasing coupling between distinct concerns.
findings.suggestion: Split `params.ts` into focused modules: `paramExtractors.ts` (read-only path resolution), `generationPayloadBuilder.ts` (write/transformation logic), and `mediaTypes.ts` (content-type mapping). Keep orchestrator and generation-child imports isolated to only the concern they actually need.
